---
title: "Michael P. Kerr"
author:
  - name: "Athens, GA"
    affiliations: "(404) 901-2505"
    email: "[michael.kerr@uga.edu](mailto:michael.kerr@uga.edu) | [github.com/mpk15956](https://github.com/mpk15956) | [linkedin.com/in/mpk15956](https://linkedin.com/in/mpk15956)"
format:
  typst:
    margin:
        x: 0.5in
        y: 0.5in
    fontsize: 11pt
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          #show heading.where(level: 1): it => block(
            width: 100%,
            stroke: (bottom: 0.5pt + black),
            inset: (bottom: 3pt),
            text(smallcaps(it.body))
          )
    page-numbering: false
abstract: |
  Computational geographer bridging topological data analysis (TDA), spatial statistics, and high-performance computing to model sustainable environmental systems. Translating high-dimensional data into interpretable inputs for predictive models to support decision-making frameworks for environmental resilience and social equity.
abstract-title: "Research Interests"
---

## Education

**The University of Georgia** `#h(1fr)`{=typst} Athens, GA \
*Bachelor of Science in Applied Mathematics and Geography* `#h(1fr)`{=typst} Expected July 2026 \
Minor: Computer Science `#h(1fr)`{=typst} GPA: 3.60/4.00 \
Certificates: Water Resources

## Relevant Coursework

- **Mathematics:** Real Analysis, Point Set Topology, Partial Differential Equations, Numerical Analysis, Probability
- **Computer Science:** Data Structures, Theory of Computing, Data Mining, Data Science and Applied Machine Learning
- **Geography:** Geospatial Artificial Intelligence, Fluvial Geomorphology, People, Planet, and Profit (Study Abroad)

## Research Experience

**Department of Mathematics, University of Georgia** `#h(1fr)`{=typst} Athens, GA \
*Directed Reading Program Student (Mentor: Cameron Thomas, PhD Candidate)* `#h(1fr)`{=typst} August 2025 -- December 2025

- Developing a reproducible, GPU-accelerated topological data analysis (TDA) pipeline in Python to extract novel, multiscale land-surface parameters from digital elevation models (DEMs).
- Research Objectives: (1) To test the hypothesis that geomorphic provinces possess distinct topological signatures, and (2) to validate whether these signatures improve predictive models of physical erosion.
- Methodology: Employing Gudhi, Dask, and PyTorch for persistent homology computation and integrating results into a predictive machine learning framework.

**Department of Agricultural and Applied Economics, University of Georgia** `#h(1fr)`{=typst} Athens, GA \
*Undergraduate Researcher (Advisor: Craig Landry, PhD)* `#h(1fr)`{=typst} July 2024 -- June 2025

- Engineered a Python analysis to process 250,000+ GPS records, quantifying green-space utilization across Athens, GA.
- Developed a spatial equity model using ArcGIS that identified critical gaps in park access for underserved communities.
- Presented findings at the CAES Undergraduate Research Symposium, incorporating feedback from faculty judges.

**Department of History and Geography, Georgia College & State University** `#h(1fr)`{=typst} Milledgeville, GA \
*Geographic Information Science Student (Advisor: Omolola Ologunorisa, PhD)* `#h(1fr)`{=typst} August 2023 -- December 2023

- Executed a site suitability analysis for optimal bike path placement by applying Morphological Spatial Pattern Analysis (MSPA) to slope, land cover, and traffic data.
- Performed network analysis on OpenStreetMap data to generate and validate routes optimized for commuter safety and multi-modal connectivity.

## Selected Presentations

- Kerr, Michael. 2025. "Analyzing Green Space Utilization in Athens, GA Through GPS Data and Spatial Analysis." Oral Presentation at the CAES Undergraduate Research Symposium, Athens, GA, April 2.

## Teaching & Leadership Experience

**Leave No Trace Level 1 Instructor Course, Atlanta Area Council, BSA** `#h(1fr)`{=typst} Blairsville, GA \
*Co-Instructor (Annual)* `#h(1fr)`{=typst} November 2021 -- Present

- Co-designed and co-led an intensive weekend-long certification course for adult leaders on Leave No Trace outdoor ethics and pedagogy.
- Developed curriculum, planned instructional activities, and facilitated training sessions for cohorts of 15--25 participants.
- Mentored aspiring instructors in pedagogical techniques for teaching environmental stewardship principles to diverse audiences.

**Langford Nature Center, Bert Adams Scout Camp, Atlanta Area Council, BSA** `#h(1fr)`{=typst} Covington, GA \
*Ecology Program Director (Promoted)* `#h(1fr)`{=typst} May 2017 -- January 2023

- Designed and standardized a comprehensive ecology curriculum for 5+ instructors and over 1,000 annual participants, creating scalable and reproducible lesson plans.
- Employed diverse pedagogical methods grounded in Kolb's Experiential Learning Model, integrating lectures, guided small-group discussions, and hands-on fieldwork.
- Led a 'train-the-trainer' workshop on Leave No Trace ethics for 80+ staff, mentoring instructors in evidence-based experiential teaching methods.

## Other Experience

**UGA Carl Vinson Institute of Government** `#h(1fr)`{=typst} Athens, GA \
*GIS Student Assistant* `#h(1fr)`{=typst} February 2024 -- May 2024

- Developed and debugged ArcPy script to automate a spatial join for 14,000+ records onto a fragmented road network.
- Eliminated manual errors and cut processing time by >99%, compressing a 6-month project in three weeks.

## Professional Affiliations

**American Association of Geographers (AAG)**, *Student Member* `#h(1fr)`{=typst} April 2024 -- Present \
**Southeastern Division of the AAG (SEDAAG)**, *Student Member* `#h(1fr)`{=typst} April 2024 -- Present

## Honors & Awards

**President's & Dean's List**, *Various Institutions* `#h(1fr)`{=typst} 2021 -- 2025 \
**HOPE Scholarship**, *State of Georgia* `#h(1fr)`{=typst} 2021 -- 2025 \
**Eagle Scout**, *Atlanta Area Council, BSA* `#h(1fr)`{=typst} December 2020

## Technical Skills

- **Programming:** Python (NumPy, Pandas, SciPy), TypeScript, R, Bash, Java, Mathematica, Matlab, Julia
- **Geospatial Analysis:** GIS Modeling (ArcGIS Pro, QGIS), DEM Processing, Network Analysis, MSPA, Geocoding
- **Machine Learning:** TDA (Persistent Homology), Regression (Scikit-learn), Computer Vision (PyTorch)
- **HPC & Data:** Distributed Computing (Dask), GPU Acceleration (CUDA/CuPy), Containerization (Docker/Podman)
